segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a medium shot of two men on a stage, illuminated by spotlights, in front of a large blue screen displaying the 'Intel | Supermicro' logo with animating pixelated squares. Ray Pang, dressed in a dark suit and glasses, actively speaks and gestures, introducing a "real Gali 3 system," humorously noting they "haven't even announced it yet" and are "a little bit ahead." The Second Speaker, in a brown blazer and glasses, listens attentively, occasionally nodding, and interjects with a brief "Okay." Ray Pang encourages the audience, visible in silhouette, to visit their booth, emphasizing that "It's real, it's not just on slide... it's a real system in flash." Alternating close-ups show Ray Pang's expressive demeanor as he addresses the Second Speaker, identified as Pat, expressing excitement about "all the new approach you guys start talking about, the system at the data center level for AI." Ray Pang continues, detailing Supermicro's expansion of their "global manufacturing footprint" to meet demand from cloud service providers and enterprise customers, stating they can deliver "5,000 fully integrated racks per month, including 1,350 liquid cooled racks." He concludes by noting that "a lot of some of those racks are Gali racks." Pat then takes over, stating, "Time to go to scale," and thanks the audience for joining, expressing excitement about the partnership before the segment ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Pat, the Second Speaker in a Dark Suit, walking across a dimly lit stage under blue spotlights, gesturing towards a massive screen displaying the 'GenAI at Intel' slide with a pixelated blue background and an image of a person holding a silicon wafer. Pat, speaking with an engaging voice, announces they will delve into 'AI PCs' and the 'magic that happens behind the scenes' in the fab, before inviting Rajshree. The screen then transitions with a 'whoosh' sound effect to a satellite view labeled 'Intel Ocotillo Campus,' which Pat confirms is 'just down the road,' before shifting to a blue grid pattern and finally a live feed from the Ocotillo Fab. On this live feed, Rajshree Sankaran, a Principal Engineer in Factory Automation, appears in a cleanroom environment, fully suited in white protective gear with a blue belt, gloves, and face mask, standing before complex machinery and a laptop. Pat continues to gesture, occasionally pointing at the live feed, and asks Rajshree if she can hear him. Rajshree responds clearly, addressing him as 'Pat' and confirming, 'Yes, I can hear you. Can you hear me?' Pat replies enthusiastically, making a lighthearted comment about the 'few thousand of my closest friends' in the audience. Rajshree then offers to show 'some of our latest AI innovations transforming our manufacturing workflow,' to which Pat responds, 'Well, fantastic, Rajshree, and Intel customer number one for our GenAI...'

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Second Speaker in a dark suit, standing on a dimly lit presentation stage with blue accents, prompting, "So, tell us more." The scene then transitions to a live feed from the Intel Ocotillo Fab, displayed on a large screen behind him, showing Rajshree Sankaran, a Principal Engineer in Factory Automation. Dressed in a full white cleanroom suit with a blue belt, gloves, face mask, and safety glasses, Rajshree stands amidst sophisticated machinery under yellow-tinted lighting, gesturing expressively with her hands. She begins to explain, "Here's one way we are using generative AI in the fab," detailing how they aim to keep equipment running by analyzing past incidents to understand root causes and resolve them quickly. She highlights the challenge, stating, "We have a huge amount of structured and unstructured data, spanning decades and millions of records," which, while useful for finding resolutions, is "time-consuming and can take hours and sometimes even days" to process manually. The Second Speaker interjects, agreeing that this "certainly isn't ideal," emphasizing the vast scale of Intel's operations with "thousands of different engineers and equipment are running daily and growing worldwide" across global fabs. Rajshree then starts to respond again, "Yeah, um, but now..." before the segment concludes.

segments.integrated_caption: In a brightly lit, yellow-hued Intel Ocotillo Fab cleanroom, Principal Engineer Rajshree Sankaran, dressed in a full white cleanroom suit with a blue belt, gloves, face mask, and safety glasses, gestures expressively as she announces, "Now we have a game changer," explaining Intel's use of LLM-based generative AI solutions to enhance productivity. She moves to a laptop displaying the 'Articul8 AI - Enterprise GenAI Platform for Intel Manufacturing,' demonstrating how to use natural language to search for equipment incidents. As she types an example, she details how the AI identifies relevant past events through semantic search and generates the most likely root cause and resolution based on historical data. Rajshree further illustrates the platform's ability to summarize steps performed to address root causes, showing work orders and identifying unique trends. This live demonstration from the Ocotillo Fab is simultaneously broadcast on a massive screen behind a Second Speaker in a dark suit, who stands on a dimly lit presentation stage with blue spotlights, presenting to an audience. The screen prominently features Rajshree's live feed on the left and the detailed Articul8 AI interface on the right, showcasing its analysis of a leak contamination incident with text responses to queries like 'What is the root cause of observed incident?' and 'Summarize the steps performed by different work orders,' powered by Xeon® and Gaudi®.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video begins with Rajshree Sankaran, a Principal Engineer in Factory Automation, standing in a brightly lit, yellow-hued cleanroom at the Intel Ocotillo Fab. She is dressed in a full white cleanroom suit with a blue belt, gloves, face mask, and safety glasses, gesturing expressively as she speaks. She moves toward a laptop on a workbench, interacting with it by typing and using the mouse, while the screen displays a text-based interface for the 'Articul8 AI - Enterprise GenAI Platform for Intel Manufacturing.' The scene then transitions to a large presentation stage where the Second Speaker in Dark Suit stands, presenting to an audience. A massive screen behind him displays a live feed of Rajshree Sankaran from the Ocotillo Fab on the left, and the Articul8 AI interface on the right. The interface shows a detailed analysis of a leak contamination incident, with text responses to queries such as 'What is the root cause of observed incident?' and 'Summarize the steps performed by different work orders.' The AI system provides detailed, structured responses, including event timelines and recommended actions, demonstrating its capability to extract insights from complex manufacturing data. The stage is dimly lit with blue spotlights, contrasting with the bright yellow cleanroom feed, emphasizing the real-time integration of factory operations with advanced AI analytics.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of the 'Articul8 AI - Enterprise GenAI Platform for Intel Manufacturing' software interface, displaying detailed text about work orders and incident analysis, including tasks like creating documents and checking for leaks. A female voice, identified as Rajshree Sankaran, explains that the platform allows users to "launch a software agent that can refine those tasks and take actions to bring the equipment back up." The interface, featuring a dark theme with sections for 'Enter Incident Details,' 'Relevant Events,' and 'Event Timeline,' transitions to a live feed from the Ocotillo Fab, showing Rajshree Sankaran herself. Dressed in a full white cleanroom suit with a blue belt, gloves, face mask, and safety glasses, she stands in a high-tech factory environment, gesturing expressively as industrial machinery and a laptop are visible behind her. The video then cuts to a wide shot of a presentation stage, where the Second Speaker in a dark suit stands, gesturing toward the large screen displaying the live feed of Rajshree and the Articul8 AI interface, which also shows 'LIVE from Ocotillo Fab' and her title 'Principal Engineer, Factory Automation.' The Second Speaker exclaims, "Wow, this is pretty incredible, Rajshree. Literally a few clicks, right? And you're getting decades of data and being able to operationalize solutions. This is incredible." Rajshree responds, "Right. And it's that simple. What I like most about this is just how much time it saves. With just a few clicks, we can identify the root cause, resolution, and even at times bring equipment back online using an LLM-based GenAI solution and our state-of-the-art factory automation software agent." The video alternates between close-ups of Rajshree in the cleanroom and the wide shot of the dimly lit stage with blue lighting, emphasizing the real-time connection. The Articul8 AI interface continues to display detailed analysis, including 'Agent Action' and 'Recommended Actions,' with text about following procedures and handling damaged components. The Second Speaker concludes, "Well, thank you, Rajshree, this is incredible. And obviously, we're doing a whole lot of things, you know, for equipment downtime, automation..." highlighting the professional and technical atmosphere and the integration of AI in manufacturing processes.

She continues, detailing how obtaining "high resolution images such as the ones that you see on the right," used for "finding the locations of defects on a die," previously "required additional processing" but is now solved by "training a cutting edge super resolution algorithm" and using "the latest generation of Intel processors for inferencing," allowing processing "directly on our laptops" to get "detailed high resolution images in under one second." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit auditorium, where the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a brown blazer over a black shirt, stands on a blue-lit stage. Behind him, a massive screen displays the 'GenAI at Intel' slide, featuring a pixelated blue background with the Intel logo and a person holding a silicon wafer. As he walks and gestures expressively, holding a small device, he speaks emphatically about "transforming our manufacturing operations," "saving millions of dollars," "accelerating our manufacturing process," and "improving yields in our factories and the productivity of our machines." He then urges the audience, "time to upgrade your AI PC fleet now," contrasting the "cool PCs" developers get with the "mediocre ones" for white-collar workers and the "crap ones" for manufacturing, before exclaiming, "No!" He points toward the screen, emphasizing the live feed from the Ocotillo Fab, which shows Rajshree Sankaran, identified as 'Principal Engineer, Factory Automation,' in a cleanroom suit, gesturing as if explaining operations. The speaker continues, "Call your head of IT, it is time for a PC AI refresh because use cases like this show extraordinary value for fleet upgrades now," highlighting the "extraordinary value" of "Xeon, Rag, Gaudi" in "delivering these AI use cases into your enterprise environment now." The camera zooms in on him as he speaks, with the background transitioning between the blue pixelated design and close-ups of the silicon wafer, maintaining a professional, tech-forward atmosphere. The audience remains seated in darkness, focused on the stage.

Dr. Crow, dressed in a dark blazer, light-colored pants, and brown shoes, walks onto the stage and shakes hands with the speaker. They stand side by side, engaging in conversation, with the screen now showing the 'intel | ASU Arizona State University' logo in white text against the same blue pixelated backdrop. The speaker greets Dr. Crow, mentioning missing him at a previous event, to which Dr. Crow responds, expressing his sadness at not being able to attend. The speaker then laughs and continues to discuss spending a day with some of the best and brightest minds in AI, some of whom were ASU graduates.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a presentation stage, where the 'intel | ASU Arizona State University' logo is prominently displayed on a large blue screen with a pixelated background. Two men stand side by side: the Second Speaker in a brown blazer and dark jeans on the left, and Dr. Michael Crow, President of Arizona State University, in a dark blazer and light pants on the right. The Second Speaker, gesturing with his hands, speaks about the unpredictable future of AI beyond two years, asking Dr. Crow about the faculty and research work around AI at ASU. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a presentation stage featuring a blue, pixelated Intel-branded backdrop, where the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a brown blazer over a black shirt and dark jeans, stands with a relaxed posture, hands slightly in his pockets. He concludes a thought about "Gen AI" and taking a "manufacturing engine to the next level." Dr. Tanja Rückert, in a shimmering white short-sleeved top and matching long skirt, with a blonde bob and a white smartwatch, then takes the lead, speaking actively and gesturing expressively with open palms, pointing, and bringing her hands together to emphasize her points. She states that "at Bosch, AI is at the heart of our products as well as our business processes," elaborating that "all products contain AI, are engineered with AI, or manufactured with AI." Dr. Rückert highlights Bosch's continuous search for partnerships, expressing happiness to "partner now with Intel to go even faster," as they are "developing" and "commercializing." She engages the audience, suggesting they "might have used, or for sure you have used Bosch AI in your car," and extends this to "mobility, industry, consumer and buildings," even asking the Second Speaker, "in your kitchen. Do you have a connected oven from Bosch?" The Second Speaker responds with a quick "Ah, not yet." Dr. Rückert playfully exclaims, "Not yet! Oh, mistake!" before explaining how a connected oven, using "anonymized data from the baking and roasting," leverages "Gen AI" to "recognize the dish." The camera alternates between wide shots of both speakers and medium close-ups focusing on Dr. Rückert, while the Intel | BOSCH logo is prominently displayed behind them. Bright stage lighting illuminates the professional setting, and an audience member is visible recording the event on a smartphone.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a presentation stage, where the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a brown blazer over a black shirt and dark jeans, stands center stage. Behind him, a massive blue screen displays a grid of partner logos including Infosys, NIQ, CtrlS, OLA KRUTRIM, IFF, BOSCH, airtel, seekr, roboflow, and Articul8, alongside a large, illuminated Circuit Board with Intel Chip. The stage is dimly lit with spotlights, and the audience is visible in shadowed rows. The speaker, with a clear and confident voice, walks across the stage while gesturing, discussing how companies need "their own models for their environment" and are "excited to train and be able to deliver that using Gaudi clusters." He gives an example of "international flavors and fragrances" innovating "molecules for perfumes," suggesting a "next perfume could be Gen AI created." He then highlights "Landing AI and Andrew Ng, one of the most well-known minds in the area of AI research," before introducing a video message from Andrew. The screen transitions to display the Intel Gaudi and LANDING AI logos, with the Circuit Board with Intel Chip remaining on the right, as the camera zooms in on the speaker. A short, distinct musical flourish signals a transition, and the scene cuts to a close-up of Andrew Ng, identified as 'Landing.AI Founder and CEO,' speaking directly to the camera in a blue-lit, modern office environment. With a clear, slightly formal voice, Andrew states he's "thrilled to be here to discuss the transformative vision revolution with large vision models," referencing the "large language model or LLM revolution." The scene then shifts to a dark background displaying an AI Advancements & Ecosystem Maturation Slide, a timeline from 2017 to 2024, showing key milestones like Transformers, Vision Transformer, GPT-3, GPT-4, and Large Vision Models (LVMs), noting that 'CV Progress Trailing Text by ~3 Years' and highlighting various AI models, applications, and Landing AI’s contributions.

segments.integrated_caption: The video begins with a dark-themed timeline slide titled 'AI Advancements & Ecosystem Maturation,' spanning from 2017 to 2024, visually charting key AI milestones with colored markers for papers, models, applications/features, and Landing AI’s contributions. The timeline highlights 'CV Progress Trailing Text by ~3 Years,' featuring labels like Transformers, GPT-3, and Large Vision Models. Andrew Ng, identified as 'Landing.AI Founder and CEO,' then appears, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office with blue walls and a circular window. In a clear, articulate voice, he explains how large language models have transformed text processing, and how the large vision model (LVM) revolution is now changing image processing. He notes a crucial difference: while internet text is similar enough to proprietary text for LLMs, business images often look nothing like internet images. This leads to a cut to a slide titled 'Business Applications Images Differ from Internet Images,' which visually contrasts internet images (people, a dog, a bridge) with specialized business images (PCBs, aerial views, pathology slides), with image sources at the bottom. Andrew Ng returns to the screen, continuing to speak with expressive hand gestures, emphasizing that LVMs adapted to specific domains are far more effective. He then introduces a quick demo of a pathology app using an LVM trained on pathology images, detailing how an LVM for histopathology was trained with over 200,000 unlabeled images on the Gaudi 2 Intel Dev Cloud cluster, scaling out to multiple nodes due to the compute-intensive nature of LVM training, thanks to the Intel Dev Cloud team.

The timeline highlights the gap between text and vision AI progress, noting 'CV Progress Trailing Text by ~3 Years,' and includes labels such as Transformers, Vision Transformer, GPT-3, GPT-4, and Large Vision Models (LVMs). segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Andrew Ng, Founder and CEO of Landing.AI, speaking directly to the camera from a modern office setting with blue walls and a circular window frame. He gestures expressively with his hands, explaining LVM training recipes and the process of running and scaling them out. His clear, explanatory voice then introduces the LVM in action, noting it's running on a Gaudi 2 machine on Intel DevCloud. The scene transitions to a screen recording of Landing AI's 'Histopathology LVM' software interface, a clean white layout with options to 'Drop Image Here' or 'Click to Upload'. Andrew Ng's narration continues as three example histopathology images are shown, and one is selected for upload. Upon submission, the interface processes the image, displaying a segmented version with nuclei highlighted in brown, demonstrating its ability to segment nuclei after fine-tuning with just a few examples. The user then selects 'Classify Tissue', and the system quickly returns a 'cancer' classification, showcasing its capability to detect cancer by fine-tuning with liver cell images. Andrew Ng's voice emphasizes that the LVM acts as a pre-built template, eliminating the need to construct new models from scratch. The video cuts back to Andrew Ng, who, maintaining eye contact and expressive hand gestures, concludes by expressing his anticipation for enterprises to unlock intelligence from image data and gain a competitive edge in the market.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video begins with Andrew Ng, identified as the Founder and CEO of Landing.AI, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting. He is wearing a light blue button-up shirt and is positioned in front of a circular window frame with blue walls and visible ceiling infrastructure in the background. He gestures with his hands as he speaks, emphasizing his points. The scene then transitions to a screen recording of a software interface titled 'Histopathology LVM' by Landing AI. The interface displays a clean, white layout with options to 'Drop Image Here' or 'Click to Upload', and downstream tasks such as 'Segment Nuclei' and 'Classify Tissue'. Three example histopathology images are shown on the left, and the user selects one to upload. Upon submission, the interface processes the image, displaying a segmented version on the right with nuclei highlighted in brown against a lighter background. The user then selects 'Classify Tissue', and the system returns a classification result: 'cancer'. The video cuts back to Andrew Ng, who continues speaking with expressive hand gestures, maintaining eye contact with the camera, reinforcing the demonstration of the AI-powered histopathology tool’s capabilities.

audio_updated_entities.Histopathology LVM: A software interface by Landing AI with a clean, white layout. It features options to upload an image, select downstream tasks ('Segment Nuclei' or 'Classify Tissue'), and displays example histopathology images. After uploading, it processes the image and displays a segmented version with nuclei highlighted in brown. The interface also returns a classification result, such as 'cancer', after selecting 'Classify Tissue'. Andrew Ng's voice explains that this tool runs on a Gaudi 2 machine on Intel DevCloud, can be fine-tuned with few examples for nuclei segmentation, and can detect cancer by fine-tuning with liver cell images, serving as a pre-built template that eliminates the need to construct new models from scratch.

He gestures with his hands while speaking, holding a small device, and the audience is visible in the foreground, as he expresses his admiration for applying AI in new domains like healthcare and research beyond text and language models, introducing Naver as a fast-growing Asian cloud service provider and a huge partner of Intel. The overall atmosphere is professional and forward-looking, emphasizing technology, global reach, and AI innovation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Soo-yeon Choi, a woman with shoulder-length dark hair, wearing a white blouse and black pants, standing in a modern, spacious office with large windows and a colorful screen behind her. She speaks directly to the camera, her voice clear and confident, introducing Naver's global presence, including its operations in the US and LINE in Japan, and its evolution from a Korean search engine to a platform with around 700 million users worldwide. She announces, "Today at Intel Vision 2024, I'm happy to share about Naver's partnership with Intel." The scene transitions to animated visuals of a futuristic cityscape and glowing data streams, symbolizing Naver’s technological ambitions, as the text 'HyperCLOVA X' appears, referencing Naver's hyper-scale GenAI model, which she states was the third globally to be announced. She emphasizes the need for "powerful and cost-effective computing power" for this model and its ecosystem, expressing excitement about "working together with Intel" and anticipating the innovations they will create for a new AI chip environment. Following her speech, which concludes with "Please stay tuned," applause and upbeat music fill the air. The scene then shifts to a large presentation stage with blue lighting and a massive screen displaying the 'Intel | NAVER' logo alongside an illuminated circuit board with an Intel chip. A man, the Second Speaker in a brown blazer over a black shirt, walks onto the stage, gesturing toward the screen. His voice is heard saying, "And in fact, you don't have to stay tuned very long. Let's uh bring on stage Dr. Ha, you know, the head of research for Naver Cloud." As he speaks, Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, identified on screen as 'Head of AI Innovation' at Naver, a man with short dark hair in a dark suit, joins him on stage amidst continued applause and music. The atmosphere is professional and forward-looking, emphasizing innovation, collaboration, and the future of AI computing.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a presentation stage under dim, blue-toned lighting, where a large screen displays the 'Intel | NAVER' logo alongside a portrait of Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, identified as 'Head of AI Innovation'. Upbeat, electronic music plays and audience applause is heard as the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, wearing a brown blazer over a black t-shirt with 'NAVER' in bright green letters, dark jeans, and glasses, walks onto the stage from the right. He exchanges a brief handshake with Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, and they stand side-by-side, facing the audience. As the music fades, the Second Speaker, with an American accent, asks, "Alright, so tell us how Intel and Naver are collaborating." The camera then zooms in on Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, who, speaking with a Korean accent, begins to explain Naver's vision as a "global sovereign LLM pioneer" and "Korea's leading tech platform company" to "develop and deploy a powerful, innovative, and safe sovereign multimodal LLM, trained with much more FLOPS." He gestures expressively with his hands—pointing, opening his palms, and clasping them—and moves slightly across the stage, maintaining eye contact. He continues, stating, "So, we decided to collaborate with Intel across three essential areas on Gaudi 2," before mentioning the first area: "we established Naver..." The background remains a solid blue with subtle pixelated patterns, reinforcing the tech-focused atmosphere, while the audience is visible in the foreground, seated in darkness, with some holding up phones to record the professional and forward-looking event highlighting the Intel and NAVER AI collaboration.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a presentation stage, where Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, dressed in a dark suit, attentively listens beside the Second Speaker. The Second Speaker, wearing a gray blazer over a 'NAVER' t-shirt and glasses, actively gestures with his right hand as he speaks, occasionally turning towards Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, indicating an interactive dialogue. He details Intel's collaboration to expand the Gaudi software ecosystem with startups and research labs, and their plans to evaluate Gaudi 2 to establish LLM training infrastructure, eventually offering commercial cloud instances to global enterprises and public sectors for the new Gen AI era. As the camera zooms in on his expressive hand movements and focused demeanor, the 'Intel | NAVER' logo is prominently displayed on the large blue, pixelated screen behind them. A second voice, likely Dr. Jung-Woo Ha, interjects, asking, "So, why did you choose Gaudi? Why us?" The Second Speaker promptly responds, calling it a "very good question" and affirming Gaudi as the "most promising and competitive option for LLM training and inference." The scene then transitions to a wider view, revealing the full stage and the dimly lit audience, some recording, as the speakers maintain eye contact and Dr. Jung-Woo Ha continues to nod, reinforcing the collaborative and engaging atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Michael Dell, identified on-screen as 'Chairman and CEO, Dell,' dressed in a dark suit over a light blue shirt. Speaking directly to the camera with a clear, articulate voice, he uses expressive hand gestures—clapping, spreading, and moving them in sync with his speech—as he discusses the rapid advances in compute, storage, networking, power, speed, automation, and intelligence. He highlights how 'all that is really come together in this moment where we're kind of moving from computation and calculation into cognition,' emphasizing that 'now we all have this sort of super genius cognitive capability right at our fingertips' and that 'every organization needs to reimagine their business given this superpower that that's being unleashed.' As the applause begins, a Man in Black Suit with Sunglasses enters from the left, carrying a sleek Silver Briefcase with black latches, which he places on the podium with a distinct sound. He then announces to Michael Dell, "Well, Michael, I think the next shipment has arrived." Michael Dell gestures and clasps his hands, maintaining a professional demeanor. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large presentation stage, revealing the Intel | DELL Technologies logo on a massive blue screen behind them. A Second Speaker in Dark Suit, wearing a gray blazer over a black t-shirt and dark jeans, stands on the right side of the stage, gesturing toward the screen as Michael Dell lifts the Silver Briefcase, revealing its white interior. The Second Speaker exclaims, "Oh wow, awesome," and then adds, "Uh, cool. I'm gonna go, I'm gonna go fire this up on our lab. Good to see you, Pat," as applause continues. The screen then cuts to a test pattern with the text 'Dell Technologies CONNECTION CLOSED' in bold white letters over a multicolored background. The scene shifts again to the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, now standing alone on stage against a dynamic blue backdrop featuring a glowing Circuit Board with an Intel Chip. He speaks animatedly, using hand gestures to emphasize his points, stating, "Well, I guess that, I guess that call is over," and then, "it's only fair to show what Michael has been excited about... I'm excited to introduce the Intel Gady 3." The background transitions to a futuristic tunnel animation with streaks of light, symbolizing high-speed data flow. The atmosphere is professional and high-tech, with spotlights illuminating the stage and the audience visible in the foreground, some recording the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a gray blazer over a black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, standing on a large presentation stage. He gestures toward a covered podium, then dramatically pulls off a black cloth to reveal a large, modular stack of circuit boards arranged in a cube-like structure, each board featuring a central square component and multiple connectors. The speaker announces, "AI accelerator for the first time. So you ready? Intel Gaudi 3," which is met with enthusiastic cheers and applause from the audience, accompanied by energetic music with lyrics like "It's just getting better." He then walks to the left side of the stage, picks up a single, smaller version of the same circuit board module, and holds it up for the audience to see, gesturing with his free hand as he speaks. The background screen transitions from a dynamic blue spiral pattern to a grid of light blue squares, reinforcing the tech-focused theme. As he continues speaking, he details the "huge advancements in generative AI," explaining that "Intel Gaudi 3" builds on Gaudi 2, delivering "4x the AI compute with BF16, 2x with FP8, 2x the networking bandwidth, 1 and a half x the memory bandwidth for massive scale out." He highlights its "significant leap in performance for AI training, popular LLMs, multi-model support," and states it's "architected from the ground up for efficient, large-scale AI computing," supporting "both scale up and scale out configurations" and performing "great on benchmarks." He moves back and forth across the stage, occasionally glancing at the large modular stack still on the podium. The stage is illuminated with blue and purple lighting, and the audience is visible in the foreground, some recording the event, contributing to the energetic and futuristic atmosphere emphasizing innovation in hardware design. The speaker’s deliberate and expressive movements underscore the significance of the hardware being presented.

segments.integrated_caption: The Second Speaker, dressed in a dark blazer over a black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, stands center stage under blue spotlights, holding a small, rectangular Single Circuit Board Module in his left hand while gesturing expressively with his right. He articulates the impressive performance of "H100 on top open llama models," highlighting a "50% faster time to train, 50% better on inferencing, and 40% better on inference power efficiency." As he speaks, occasionally turning toward the screen, the background transitions from an abstract grid of light blue squares to a close-up, angled view of a Circuit Board with an Intel Chip, showcasing intricate circuitry and memory modules, visually reinforcing his technical points. He emphasizes "a scalable solution with Gaudi 3 using industry standard Ethernet," detailing how performance scales "from one socket to an eight accelerator UBB, a single node, up to thousands of nodes and racks." With open palms and pointing gestures, he addresses a key customer question: "how do I deploy?" explaining that "nearly all of the Gen AI developments today are moving to higher level environments, PyTorch frameworks and other community models from Hugging Face." He asserts that the industry is "quickly moving away from proprietary CUDA models," promising that with "literally a few lines of code," users can be "up and running with industry standard frameworks on power performant efficient Gaudi infrastructure," all while the detailed circuit board imagery on screen underscores the advanced semiconductor technology being discussed.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a dark blazer, black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, holding a Single Circuit Board Module in his left hand while gesturing with his right as he begins to discuss the "Intel Gaudi 3." He explains its availability to OEMs starting this quarter and its "three different industry standard form factors," referring to the module he holds as "the accelerator card that I was just showing off here to you." The camera then pulls back to a wide shot of the professional, tech-focused Presentation Stage with Audience, revealing the speaker to the right of a large podium displaying a Modular Circuit Board Stack. Behind him, a massive screen shows the 'Intel® Gaudi® 3 AI Accelerator' slide, detailing the Mezzanine Card, Universal Baseboard, and PCIe CEM. The speaker moves around the stage, occasionally pointing to the screen and the physical stack, emphasizing the modular design and introducing a "PCIe card and finally, our Universal Baseboard," which he affectionately calls "this big boy" and "Big Brother." He highlights its impressive specifications, including "up to 14.6 FP8 petaflops," "greater than a terabyte of HBM3E," and "192 native 200 gigabit Ethernet connections." Returning to the podium, he places the Single Circuit Board Module beside the Modular Circuit Board Stack, continuing to gesture toward it and explaining the "extraordinary compute density" of the Gaudi PCIe card, offering "great flexibility" to customers. The audience, some recording, is visible under dim lighting with spotlights on the engaging speaker.

segments.integrated_caption: The Second Speaker, dressed in a dark suit, black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, stands on a presentation stage, holding a Single Circuit Board Module in his left hand. He uses expressive right-hand gestures to emphasize his points as he details the "Gaudy 3 generation," highlighting its "1.8 petaflops of FP8 capabilities, 128 gigabytes of memory in 600 watt TDP," and its role as an "easy adjunct to a Xeon deployment." Behind him, a large screen displays a close-up, angled view of a Circuit Board with Intel Chip, emphasizing intricate circuitry and rows of memory modules, visually reinforcing the advanced technology he describes. He explains how this technology delivers "industry standard infrastructure" and "eliminates vendor lock-in with standard networking solutions" for data centers and cloud environments, all within "open software environments." As he speaks, the screen transitions to a dynamic blue abstract design with glowing lines, enhancing the futuristic atmosphere. He gestures toward a Modular Circuit Board Stack on a podium to his right, further illustrating the hardware's design. The speaker emphasizes "huge TCO advantages" and declares it a "winning solution," concluding with "Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Gaudy 3 generation," which is met with enthusiastic applause. He then adds that "the momentum is building, the roadmap is strong," and they are seeing an "acceleration of high performance computing."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Pat, the Second Speaker in a Dark Suit, standing on stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks about "executing of AI workloads of Intel's Xeon and our next generation Gaudi 3 solutions." Behind him, a large screen displays a detailed, illuminated Circuit Board with an Intel Chip, glowing with blue and green light, set against a dynamic, pixelated blue background. Pat, wearing a dark blazer over a black t-shirt and dark jeans, holds a small device in his right hand and uses his left hand to emphasize points, noting the "growing momentum in the industry of our partners, our solutions and customers who are now saying, 'Man, this thing is great.'" He then announces, "So let's put it all together for our discussion today. And let's bring Brian on stage and we're going to show just an enterprise application or two here to finish up our time together this morning." As he speaks, the scene transitions to a full-screen slide showcasing a grid of partner logos, including Infosys, IBM, airtel, BOSCH, Lenovo, and Dell Technologies, arranged neatly on a dark blue background with a glowing circuit board graphic. The video then cuts to a new scene where Pat stands beside Brian, the Man in an Intel Polo Shirt, at a podium with three Laptops branded with Intel, Lenovo, and MSI logos. Brian, with a beard, gestures animatedly while speaking, responding to Pat's question, "you got anything good to show us?" with "I got a couple things Pat." Brian then references Pat's earlier discussion about "LLMs, chatbots, rags and all that" and promises to "bring this all together and show you a cool enterprise Edge AI use case here that every single one of you here in the audience are going to relate to. Email. You get hundreds of emails, especially to put on a show like this." Pat listens attentively, occasionally responding, against a solid blue screen backdrop with a partial white logo visible on the right.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a medium shot of two men on a stage with a vibrant blue, pixelated background. The man on the left, wearing a light gray Intel polo shirt, gestures animatedly while speaking, asking how 'artificial intelligence' can help get through tasks quicker, specifically mentioning 'inbox zero.' The man on the right, in a dark blazer, interjects, calling 'Inbox zero, the holy grail.' The man in the Intel polo shirt continues, introducing Outlook and explaining how 'GenPilot Service' has developed a solution. As he speaks, the camera zooms out to a wide shot, revealing a large screen displaying an Outlook email interface. The screen shows an email titled 'Question about Intel Xeon' with a 'GenPilot Service Pilot' sidebar generating a 'Suggested Reply' summarizing the Intel Xeon Platinum 8570 processor's features. The man in the Intel polo shirt details how this provides 'all the information specific to the product' without needing to go through 'spec sheets' or 'databases,' and how simply hitting 'compose' creates a ready-to-go email. A close-up of the screen shows the generated email draft. The camera returns to the stage as the man in the blazer gestures toward the screen, remarking, 'In just seconds. That's pretty impressive.' The audience responds with enthusiastic applause, highlighting the professional and tech-focused atmosphere of the event, with spotlights illuminating the stage and the audience visible in the foreground, some recording.
segments.visual_original_caption: The segment opens with a medium shot of two men standing behind a podium on a stage with a vibrant blue, pixelated background. The man on the left, wearing a light gray Intel polo shirt, gestures animatedly while speaking to the man on the right, who is dressed in a dark blazer over a black t-shirt and dark jeans, holding a small device in his right hand. On the podium are three laptops—branded MSI, Lenovo, and another Intel device—open and facing forward. The man in the blazer listens intently, occasionally nodding and responding. The camera then zooms out to a wide shot, revealing the full stage with a large screen displaying an Outlook email interface. segments.integrated_caption: The presentation opens with a wide shot of a stage bathed in blue lights, where Brian, the Man in an Intel Polo Shirt, stands at a podium alongside the Second Speaker in a Dark Suit. A large screen behind them displays the Intel Vision logo and a split view: an architectural sketch of the 'Ohio facility' on the left, and a ComfyUI interface on the right. Brian points to the sketch, questioning why it's a 'rainy scenario' when they are in 'Sunny Phoenix,' and announces his intention to change it to 'sunny.' The Second Speaker confirms the 'Ohio facility' as Brian explains that he will 'queue the prompt,' promising the image will come to life 'in just seconds' with 'very little to no effort.' The camera zooms in on the ComfyUI interface as Brian clicks 'Queue Prompt.' The Second Speaker humorously remarks about 'live demos' as the GPU usage monitor for an Intel(R) Arc(TM) Graphics card spikes. The photorealistic rendering of a modern building in a park instantly transforms from a dark, overcast scene to a bright, sunny one. Brian exclaims with excitement, 'There we go! Oh, there we go!' The Second Speaker acknowledges the successful render. Brian then elaborates on the AI's efficiency, highlighting that it didn't take 'billions of dollars and years' and that with '10 extra seconds of effort,' AI produced a photorealistic render of the 'Ohio fab' still under construction. The Second Speaker praises Brian, calling the demonstration 'great.' The camera then zooms back out, showing Brian gesturing towards the Intel, Lenovo, and MSI branded laptops on the podium, with the audience visible in the foreground, some recording the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up on Brian, the Man in Intel Polo Shirt, who, with an energetic voice, declares, "All right, well, you know, I know that we had some fun with this, you know, but again, the whole thing is about productivity. And I'm here to show the case for the demo guy." He actively engages with three Intel, Lenovo, and MSI branded laptops on a podium, gesturing towards them before lifting one to display a basketball player image on its screen. Beside him, Pat, the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, stands holding a small device. The scene transitions to a wide shot, revealing the full stage where a large screen behind them magnifies the basketball player image, emphasizing the laptop’s display quality. Brian continues, "So yesterday I was looking for you, Pat, for the rehearsal and they said that he was at an at a sporting event. So, you know, this the whole thing was, okay, what if Pat was here? Well, no, what if I was with Pat? Um, and then I came up with this, you know, I ran an LLM on my on my system," which elicits laughter from the audience and Pat. Brian then asks, "So, what do you say, Pat?" to which Pat, with a slightly deeper, more measured voice, responds, "I think this demo, I think we're done." After a brief exchange and Brian's request for "some many IPCs for the demo guys," Pat confirms, "Absolutely. Absolutely. Okay, thank you, Brian," as the audience applauds. Pat then takes center stage, walking confidently as the screen changes to display 'Today Age of AI Co-pilots' and then expands to a full slide titled 'The Age of AI Acceleration,' outlining a timeline from 'Today' to 'Future' with stages like 'Age of AI Co-pilots,' 'Age of AI Agents,' and 'Age of AI Functions.' He gestures toward the screen, explaining, "Yeah, I would have loved to be helping the boilermakers last night, but my jump shot, you know, okay," followed by more laughter, before continuing, "You know, everything I've talked about today, it's not gonna happen overnight. But it's also not gonna take long. You know, we see this enterprise..." The audience is visible in the dimly lit auditorium below, while the stage lighting, with its blue pixelated backdrop and spotlights, maintains a professional, tech-forward atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a dimly lit auditorium, where the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, dressed in a dark blazer, black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, stands under blue spotlights on stage. Behind him, a massive screen displays 'The Age of AI Acceleration' slide, featuring a dynamic tunnel animation and a timeline of AI progression. Holding a small device, the speaker confidently walks and gestures expressively, explaining how "AI transformation" is "unfolding in three different stages." He elaborates on the first stage, "the age of the AI co-pilot," describing it as a "really smart thing helping me do my job better," automating, streamlining, and optimizing tasks, expecting "productivity improvements of maybe 25%." As he speaks, the camera alternates between wide shots of the audience and close-ups of his animated delivery, with the blue pixelated background and the AI timeline consistently visible. He then transitions to the second stage, "the age of agents," which he states "is nigh upon us," where "the AI automates entire workflows," likening it to an "autonomous car" where one "let go of the wheel." He anticipates enterprises developing "domain specific models" and "agents" that "program to act autonomously," citing examples like "check out agents and compliance and customer service, automatically partnering with us 7x24." The professional and forward-looking atmosphere is underscored by his confident demeanor and the futuristic visuals.

He holds a small device, gestures with his right hand as he speaks, and walks across the stage to present the 'The Age of AI Acceleration' slide. His gestures become increasingly expansive, including outstretched arms, as he emphasizes the future of AI, speaking with a confident and declarative tone about AI's cognitive capabilities, the demand for "10,000 times more computing power" for AGI this decade, the pursuit of "power efficient computing" as "stewards of Moore's Law," and the transformative power of Silicon, concluding that "every company's going to become an AI company" and detailing how they will "unleash the power of your data," "accelerate your productivity," "enable your workforce," and "vastly improve the ROI of your technology investments," all while making it "more sustainable and secure."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, a man in a dark blazer over a black t-shirt, dark jeans, and glasses, standing on stage against a solid blue background, speaking into a small microphone clipped to his collar. He states, "We were made for moments like this. Together with all of you, we're going to change the world again. Thank you." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the dimly lit Presentation Stage with Audience, featuring blue spotlights and a massive screen displaying 'FUTURE' over a pixelated design, as the audience erupts in applause. An upbeat, futuristic musical score begins, accompanying a dynamic, cinematic montage. This montage showcases a futuristic cityscape at dusk with glowing light trails, a close-up of a woman’s eye with a digital overlay, a child in a high-tech suit looking up at a flying aircraft, a woman and child gazing at Earth from a spacecraft window, and a futuristic city built atop a glowing circuit board. A female voiceover narrates over the music, "Now that you've seen what the future might hold, with AI-enabled enterprise gold, automotive, financial, shipping, and more. With the right Woohoo chips, every business can soar. Success will be boundless, the future so bright, you'll have to wear shades well into the night. Intel's enterprise solutions, bringing AI everywhere." The montage culminates in a panoramic view of a city emerging from clouds with digital symbols floating above. The scene cuts back to the stage, now dark, with the text 'BUNNY REMIX DJ' appearing in large white letters. The stage lights shift to purple and blue as the screen displays 'DJ BUNNY REMIX' and then a stylized, animated video of a DJ performing in a futuristic environment with swirling, colorful graphics. The text 'AI DJ Bunny intelVISION' appears in the bottom left corner, as upbeat electronic dance music with a female vocal sample ("Hey, look up, look up, the sun is shining bright today. No clouds in sight, everything's looking okay.") plays. The DJ, wearing a white hoodie and cap, is shown manipulating controls on a sleek console, surrounded by vibrant, abstract visuals that pulse and flow in sync with the music. The camera zooms in on the DJ’s hands and then pulls back to show the full stage, where the audience is visible in the foreground, some holding up phones to record the immersive, high-energy performance, filled with light beams and digital patterns.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment kicks off with AI DJ Bunny, a stylized, futuristic character in a white hoodie, cap, and large goggles, energetically manipulating a sleek DJ console within a high-tech, dark, abstract stage. Vibrant, pulsating visuals of neon lines, swirling patterns, and abstract shapes in electric blues, pinks, and purples are projected on large vertical screens, synchronized with the driving, upbeat electronic dance music featuring a female vocalist. The lyrics, including phrases like "you can't help but smile," "I've got a brand new feeling," and "I'm feeling good," amplify the immersive, cyberpunk atmosphere as the DJ moves rhythmically, adjusting controls and gesturing to the beat. The camera then pulls back, revealing this dynamic performance displayed on a massive screen to a dimly lit live auditorium, with some audience members recording. The music continues with an encouraging tone, singing "it just gets better," "don't wanna miss this," and "take the future, bring it home with," before the screen transitions to a solid blue background, prominently displaying the pixelated white and light blue 'intel VISION' logo, marking the conclusion of the performance and the event's branding.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a dynamic, animated performance by AI DJ Bunny, a stylized, futuristic DJ character wearing a white hoodie, cap, and large goggles, manipulating a sleek DJ console in a high-tech environment. The setting is a dark, abstract stage with vibrant, pulsating visuals—neon lines, swirling patterns, and abstract shapes in electric blues, pinks, and purples—projected on large vertical screens flanking the DJ. The visuals shift rapidly, synchronized with the music, creating an immersive, cyberpunk atmosphere. The DJ moves rhythmically, adjusting controls, raising arms, and gesturing to the beat, fully immersed in the performance. As the sequence progresses, the camera pulls back to reveal the performance is being displayed on a massive screen in a live auditorium, with a dimly lit audience visible below, some recording the event. The screen transitions to a solid blue background with the 'intel VISION' logo in white and light blue, featuring a pixelated design, marking the end of the performance segment and the beginning of the event’s branding phase.

entity_library.Split-Screen Text Display: A large screen showing two side-by-side 'ChatBot' windows, demonstrating the performance of a 4th Gen Intel® Xeon® Processor running 'Llama 2 70B' in FP16 (implying 8-bit) and 4-bit formats, with latency metrics displayed, as the speaker explains the AI model inference task.
entity_library.Medical Imaging Interface: A large screen background displaying multiple brain scans and data panels, suggesting AI applications in healthcare diagnostics and telemedicine.
entity_library.Intel Core Ultra Laptop Slide: A full-screen presentation slide featuring the 'Intel Core Ultra' logo, the text 'AI PC Momentum', shipment metrics (>5M AI PCs Shipped to Date and 40Mu Shipped by End of This Year), and an image of a silver laptop with a vibrant blue and purple abstract wallpaper. A confident male speaker discusses the slide's content, confirming the displayed shipment figures, detailing Intel's AI PC platform evolution, the rapid ramp-up of Core Ultra, the launch of commercial versions, expansion of developer programs, optimization of over 500 AI models, and partnerships with OEMs like Dell, HP, and Lenovo, while also mentioning future plans including 'Lunar Lake'.
entity_library.Lunar Lake Slide: A presentation slide introducing 'Lunar Lake (codename)' as the 'Flagship SoC for Next Gen of AI PCs', featuring key performance claims: '3X AI Performance' and '>100 Platform TOPS', which the speaker highlights as a significant leap, contrasting Intel's second generation with competitors' first.
entity_library.Bringing AI EVERYWHERE Slide: A presentation slide titled 'Bringing AI EVERYWHERE' that displays three tiers of AI infrastructure for 'ENTERPRISE & EDGE' and 'DATA CENTER', shown on the large blue screen while the speaker discusses data centers and AI innovation.
entity_library.Laws of the EDGE Slide: A presentation slide titled 'Laws of the EDGE' listing 'Economics', 'Physics', and 'Land' as key factors, with a detailed image of a Circuit Board with Intel Chip on the right side, glowing with blue light, which the speaker refers to as 'the three laws' while discussing the increasing importance of edge computing.
entity_library.Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Sli: A presentation slide titled 'Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)' that visually contrasts 'Enterprise Data' (mature, more secure and confidential, x86 CPU based) with 'AI Models' (rapidly evolving and very disruptive, accelerator based). The speaker elaborates on its central diagram, explaining RAG as a 'perfect bridge' connecting 'Embedding and Vectorization' between LLMs and big data repositories, and highlighting its role in combining modern open-source LLMs with real-time data from vector and unstructured databases.
entity_library.Woman at Podium: A woman named Diana with shoulder-length grayish hair, wearing a black blazer over a light gray collared shirt, seated at a podium with a laptop, actively typing and demonstrating the RAG system. She is praised by an off-screen voice as 'the best'.
entity_library.Open Platform for Enterprise AI Slide: A presentation slide displayed on a large blue screen, featuring a grid of partner logos including Red Hat, Yellowbrick, Redis, Zilliz, SAP, VMware by Broadcom, and others, set against a blue background with pixelated patterns, symbolizing a collaborative ecosystem for enterprise AI, which the speaker refers to as the 'open platform' and 'ecosystem' in his presentation.
entity_library.Lan Guan: A woman with shoulder-length dark hair, wearing a light blue suit with white pants, a dark beaded necklace, and rings on her fingers. She speaks expressively, using hand gestures to emphasize her points, and moves slightly as she talks, maintaining eye contact with the audience and her co-speaker. Her voice is confident and articulate as she discusses implementing responsible AI solutions and the continuous reinvention of GenAI, engaging the audience with a question about embarking on an exciting journey. She later responds 'My pleasure. Thank you.' to the Second Speaker.
entity_library.Intel | Accenture Logo: A large, white logo displayed on a blue screen behind the speakers, featuring the text 'intel | accenture' with a vertical line separating the names. The background includes a dynamic, pixelated pattern of light blue squares that subtly shift and move, adding visual interest to the branding.
entity_library.Intel® Xeon® 6 Processor Slide: A presentation slide titled 'Intel® Xeon® 6 Processor: Performance & Efficiency' featuring a glowing, tree-like circuit board design with blue and white lights, symbolizing advanced technology. The slide includes the Intel logo and a disclaimer at the bottom, visually supporting the speaker's discussion of a '6.4X improvement from Gen 4 to Xeon 6' on a '70 billion parameter' model, specifically mentioning 'Llama 2 70B parameters' and the ability to run it in data centers without 'expensive proprietary stuff'.
entity_library.Silicon Wafer: A circular, dark red silicon wafer with a grid pattern, held up by the Second Speaker in Dark Suit to showcase its intricate design. The speaker identifies it as "our Granite Rapids Intel 3 wafer," which will be launching "shortly after the Sierra Forest product" on "Intel 3 process technology." It is later placed on the podium next to an identical wafer.
entity_library.Maximizing the Power of YOUR Data Slide: A presentation slide with the title 'Maximizing the Power of YOUR Data' displayed on a large screen, featuring a futuristic cityscape with glowing blue lines and structures, symbolizing data connectivity and advanced technology. The speaker explicitly discusses "your data" and "your data stack" while this slide is visible.
entity_library.Podium with Laptops and Server: A large, rectangular podium on stage with three laptops and a server unit, each displaying a 'ChatBot' interface, used to demonstrate real-time AI model inference performance with Intel® Xeon® processors. The podium features a blue and white pixelated design matching the stage's aesthetic, and is implicitly referred to by the speaker as a 'Gen 5 machine' during a demonstration comparing processor generations.
entity_library.Data is Your MOST Valuable Asset: A presentation slide with the bold text 'Data is Your MOST Valuable Asset' displayed on a large screen, accompanied by an image of two professionals in a data center environment, one holding a laptop, visually reinforcing the speaker's emphasis on 'unleashing the most dramatic source of business already available' by taking advantage of data in data centers and databases.
entity_library.CONFIDENTIAL Computing & AI: A presentation slide titled 'CONFIDENTIAL Computing & AI' listing 'Intel® Trust Domain Extensions (Intel® TDX)', 'Extended to AI Workloads with Nvidia and soon Gaudi3', and 'Intel® Trust Services', with a note that it is 'Available in the Cloud With Major Cloud Service Providers', displayed on a large screen with a background image of a secure computing environment showing hands typing and a shield icon on a monitor. This slide provides the visual context for the speaker's discussion on enterprise AI adoption and the need for cost-effective, high-performance generative AI solutions.
entity_library.Intel eTHERNET Logo: A prominent blue and white logo displayed on a large screen, featuring the text 'Intel eTHERNET' with a pixelated design, serving as a central branding element for the presentation segment, which the speaker references as he discusses the evolution and future of Ethernet solutions.

The speaker explicitly mentions "UEC" (Ultra Ethernet Consortium) as Intel's vehicle for "revolutionizing" Ethernet networking for AI fabrics.
entity_library.Futuristic Tunnel Animation: A dynamic, high-tech animation on the right side of the screen, depicting a glowing blue tunnel with streaks of light and floating dots, symbolizing rapid data flow and connectivity. This animation visually reinforces the speaker's points about "AI fabrics" and "high-speed data transmission" for future AI solutions.
entity_library.Complete AI Systems Strategy Slide: A presentation slide titled 'Complete AI Systems Strategy' featuring a 3D cube labeled 'AI' with four attributes listed: Open & Easy, Secure & Responsible, Scalable & Reliable, and Accessible, displayed on a large blue screen with pixelated patterns.
entity_library.Delivering Cutting-edge AI APPLICATIONS: A presentation slide titled 'Delivering Cutting-edge AI APPLICATIONS' showing an image of a woman in a light blue shirt working at a desk with multiple monitors, one displaying a complex data visualization, symbolizing advanced AI application development.
entity_library.Ray Pang: A man in a dark suit with a white shirt and glasses, standing on stage and speaking with expressive hand gestures. He is the primary speaker, introducing the 'world's first Gali 3 system,' detailing Supermicro's manufacturing expansion, and discussing AI systems at the data center level.
entity_library.Intel | Supermicro Logo: A large, white logo displayed on a blue screen behind the speakers, featuring the text 'intel | SUPERMICRO' with a vertical line separating the names, set against a dynamic, pixelated background of light blue squares.
entity_library.GenAI at Intel Slide: A presentation slide displayed on the large screen, featuring the text 'GenAI at Intel' in white on a blue background with pixelated patterns, alongside an image of a person in a lab coat holding a silicon wafer, symbolizing AI innovation and semiconductor technology. Its theme aligns with the speaker's later discussion of 'best and brightest minds in AI'.
entity_library.Intel Ocotillo Campus: A satellite image of a large industrial complex labeled 'Intel Ocotillo Campus,' showing detailed aerial views of buildings, roads, and infrastructure, displayed on the large screen during the presentation.
entity_library.Rajshree Sankaran: A woman in a full white cleanroom suit with a blue belt, gloves, face mask, and safety glasses, standing in a high-tech factory environment, identified on-screen as 'Principal Engineer, Factory Automation.' She appears in a live feed from the Ocotillo Fab, gesturing expressively, her presence serving as a direct visual example of the AI use cases the speaker is promoting.
entity_library.Live Feed from Ocotillo Fab: A real-time video feed displayed on the large screen, showing Rajshree Sankaran in a cleanroom environment. The speaker uses this live demonstration to underscore the "extraordinary value" of AI use cases in manufacturing, making it a key visual example for his arguments.
entity_library.Articul8 AI - Enterprise GenAI Platform: A software interface displayed on a laptop and large screen, featuring a dark theme with text input fields and responses. It includes sections like 'Enter Incident Details,' 'Relevant Events,' and 'Event Timeline,' and provides detailed AI-generated analysis of manufacturing incidents, such as root cause identification and recommended actions. The interface is powered by Xeon® and Gaudi® and is used to demonstrate multi-level insight generation from manufacturing datasets, as explained by Rajshree Sankaran, who details its capabilities for natural language search, root cause analysis, resolution summarization, and trend identification.
entity_library.Microscopic Silicon Images: A black-and-white split-screen showing four high-magnification images of silicon structures or defects, likely from a scanning electron microscope. The top two images show irregular, textured surfaces, while the bottom two display patterned, grid-like structures with features such as trenches and vias. A disclaimer at the bottom reads: 'Visit intel.com/performanceindex for workloads and configurations. Results may vary.' Rajshree Sankaran's voice refers to these as 'high resolution images' used for 'finding the locations of defects on a die'.
entity_library.Dr. Michael Crow: The President of Arizona State University, dressed in a dark blazer, light-colored pants, and brown shoes, who engages in conversation, listening attentively and occasionally responding with hand gestures. He expresses his excitement, finding it 'stunning how you're able to... expand the potential workforce by using AI' and later states, 'we are so excited about the Intel ASU partnership,' highlighting Intel's role as an 'innovative, driving force.'
entity_library.intel | ASU Arizona State University Log: A large, white logo displayed on a blue screen behind the speakers, featuring the text 'intel | ASU Arizona State University' with a vertical line separating the names, set against a dynamic, pixelated background of light blue squares, serving as the backdrop for a discussion about the partnership's innovative work.
entity_library.Dr. Tanja Rückert: A woman in a shimmering white short-sleeved top and matching long skirt, with a blonde bob and a white smartwatch on her left wrist, actively speaking with expressive hand gestures such as open palms, pointing, and bringing her hands together to emphasize her points. She occasionally turns toward the Second Speaker in Dark Suit as she speaks, her voice carrying a clear German accent as she discusses synthetic training data and expresses enthusiasm for the journey ahead, concluding with "Thank you very much. Thanks everybody."
entity_library.Intel | BOSCH Logo: A large, white logo displayed on a blue screen behind the speakers, featuring the text 'Intel | BOSCH' with a vertical line separating the names, set against a dynamic, pixelated background of light blue squares that subtly shift and move, adding visual interest to the branding. The Second Speaker explicitly mentions "Intel, Bosch" during his presentation.
entity_library.Andrew Ng: A man in a light blue button-up shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting with blue walls and a circular window frame, gesturing with his hands as he speaks, emphasizing his points, and maintaining eye contact with the viewer. He discusses the efficiency of large vision models running on the Intel Dev Cloud.
entity_library.AI Advancements & Ecosystem Maturation S: A dark-themed timeline slide titled 'AI Advancements & Ecosystem Maturation,' spanning from 2017 to 2024, showing key AI milestones with colored markers for papers (orange), models (white), applications/features (blue), and Landing AI’s contributions (blue with a gear icon). The timeline highlights the gap between text and vision AI progress, noting 'CV Progress Trailing Text by ~3 Years,' and includes labels such as Transformers, Vision Transformer, GPT-3, GPT-4, and Large Vision Models (LVMs).
entity_library.Business Applications Images Differ from: A slide titled 'Business Applications Images Differ from Internet Images,' which compares images from the Internet (e.g., people, a dog, a bridge) with those from business applications like PCBs, aerial views, and pathology slides. The slide includes image sources at the bottom.
entity_library.Histopathology LVM: A software interface by Landing AI with a clean, white layout. It features options to upload an image, select downstream tasks ('Segment Nuclei' or 'Classify Tissue'), and displays example histopathology images. After uploading, it processes the image and displays a segmented version with nuclei highlighted in brown. The interface also returns a classification result, such as 'cancer', after selecting 'Classify Tissue'. Andrew Ng's voice explains that this tool runs on a Gaudi 2 machine on Intel DevCloud, can be fine-tuned with few examples for nuclei segmentation, and can detect cancer by fine-tuning with liver cell images, serving as a pre-built template that eliminates the need to construct new models from scratch.
entity_library.Soo-yeon Choi: A woman with shoulder-length dark hair, wearing a white blouse and black pants, standing in a modern office environment with large windows and a colorful screen behind her. She speaks directly to the camera, her voice clear and confident, introducing Naver's global presence, its evolution, the announcement of 'HyperCLOVA X' as Naver's hyper-scale GenAI model, and expressing excitement about the partnership with Intel for powerful and cost-effective computing power.
entity_library.Intel | NAVER Logo: A large, white logo displayed on a blue screen behind the speakers, featuring the text 'Intel | NAVER' with a vertical line separating the names, set against a dynamic, pixelated background of light blue squares that subtly shift and move, adding visual interest to the branding.
entity_library.Dr. Jung-Woo Ha: A man with short dark hair, wearing a dark suit, standing beside the Second Speaker in Dark Suit, listening attentively, occasionally nodding, and maintaining eye contact, and likely asking the question, 'So, why did you choose Gaudi?
